title: Do not use "true" after "syslog" in exit commands
body:
Instead of being interpreted as an argument to the boolean flag, the 'true' is being intepreted as the Podman command to be run - so we're trying to run `podman true`, which does not exist. This causes the cleanup command to fail when `--log-level=debug` is set, so containers are not cleaned up or removed.

Separate, but worth looking into later: the errors we get on trying `podman true` with any flags are terrible - if you just type `podman true` you get a quite sane "Unrecognized command" error, but if you try `podman true --rm` you get an "unknown flag --rm" error - which makes very little sense given the command itself doesn't exist.

@edsantiago PTAL, I finally tracked down the `podman run --log-level=debug -d --rm` errors.

Nice catch!

Was there ever an issue filed about this? I can't seem to find one. And, what would a test for this look like?

I can't think of a test that isn't horribly racey - with `--rm` you can normally rely on the container being gone after the command exits, but that's out the window with `-d`. We could do a 5 second sleep and then check if it still exists, but that might not be enough on slow CI systems.

For an issue, I looked but could not find one.
Sounds about right. But for posterity, would you mind editing the description and adding the reproducer? It would be nice to have for future reference, but I no longer remember that far back.
(I edited the commit message not the description)
The podman true versus podman true --rm, seems likely to be a problem in cobra?

> The podman true versus podman true --rm, seems likely to be a problem in cobra?

Whether the flag is anchored to a command vs. global also comes into play here.  I suspect --syslog=true would have also resolved the issue.  This also came up during the --remote support, which we anchored to the podman command vs. global.

`--syslog=true` would probably work, but these flags go through multiple levels of parsing (they get passed through Conmon first via `--exit-command-arg`) so I try to avoid making them any more complex than they have to be.
